doc: CHECK-validity parity ledger. One entry per CLAN CHECK error number we have
grounded against a real fixture. Both sides evolve independently (Leonid on
CLAN CHECK, us on chatter), so two tests guard it:
- chatter_matches_check (CI, no CLAN needed): asserts chatter's validation
behaviour on each fixture matches `status`. Catches OUR drift.
- clan_check_grounding (#[ignore], CLAN-gated): runs real CLAN CHECK on each
fixture and asserts it still emits `check_code`. Catches LEONID's drift;
re-run it when a new CLAN bundle lands.
parity     = chatter flags an equivalent error; assert `expected_chatter_codes` present.
gap        = CLAN flags it but chatter does NOT yet; assert chatter validates the
fixture as VALID. Flip to parity (with the new code) when implemented.
divergence = intentional: chatter handles it differently; assert `expected_chatter_codes`.

entries.check_message: Expected characters are: @ or %% or *.
entries.note: First substantive byte is an invalid top-level line start (`x`) before an otherwise ordinary CHAT file. CLAN emits CHECK 1; chatter rejects the bad line start with E316.

entries.check_message: Missing ':' character and argument.
entries.note: A complete CHAT preamble is followed by a main tier whose speaker code is not terminated by a colon. CLAN emits CHECK 2; chatter rejects the malformed line with E316.

entries.check_message: Missing word after code.
entries.note: Retrace marker [/] with nothing after it. chatter E370 StructuralOrderError.

entries.check_message: "@ID" header must immediately follow "@Participants:" or "@Options" header.
entries.note: A changeable header (@Comment) sits between @Participants and the @ID block.

entries.check_message: Header must follow "@ID:" or "@Birth of" or "@Birthplace of" or "@L1 of" header.
entries.note: A changeable header sits between the @ID block and @Birth of.

entries.check_message: Duplicate speaker declaration.
entries.note: Same speaker declared twice in @Participants. chatter E549 DuplicateSpeakerDeclaration.

entries.check_message: Commas at the end of PARTICIPANTS tier are not allowed.
entries.note: Trailing comma at end of @Participants. The grammar already surfaces the dangling comma as a tree-sitter ERROR node inside the participants header; the parser reports it as E550 TrailingCommaInParticipants (parse-stage diagnostic). CLAN CHECK 100.

entries.check_message: Only one "@Begin" can be in a file.
entries.note: Two @Begin headers. chatter rejects with E501 (Duplicate @Begin header). Behavioral grounding overturns the name-matcher audit, which marked this a gap.

entries.check_message: Space character is not allowed before comma(,) character on "@Media:" header.
entries.note: Space before the comma in @Media makes the header malformed; chatter rejects it (E525 malformed header, plus E330). Both validators reject; the precise space-before-comma message is a CLAN nicety chatter does not replicate.

entries.check_message: The file must start with "@Begin" tier.
entries.note: No @Begin header. chatter rejects with E504 (Missing required @Begin header in file preamble). Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 43; audit name-matcher had marked it a gap.

entries.check_message: "@Participants:" tier is expected here.
entries.note: No @Participants header. chatter rejects with E504 (Missing required @Participants header), plus E543/E522. Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 61.

entries.check_message: Missing Corpus name.
entries.note: Empty corpus field (2nd) in @ID. chatter E514 EmptyIDCorpus. Root-caused: corpus was wrongly Option<CorpusName>; made it a required CorpusName like its siblings, the Validate trait flags empty. CLAN CHECK 63.

entries.check_message: Tabs should only be used to mark the beginning of lines.
entries.note: A tab in the middle of a main tier. chatter rejects it (E370, unexpected tab). Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 132.

entries.check_message: "@Options" header must immediately follow "@Participants:" header.
entries.note: @Options placed after @ID instead of immediately after @Participants. chatter E551 OptionsHeaderOutOfOrder, mirroring the @ID rule (E548/CHECK 126). CLAN CHECK 125.

entries.check_message: Duplicate speaker declaration.
entries.note: Two @ID lines naming the same speaker (CHI). Distinct from the @Participants-duplicate variant (CHECK_013_duplicate_speaker.cha): CLAN 13 fires for duplicate @ID lines too, which chatter previously missed. Now E549 via check_duplicate_id_headers. CLAN CHECK 13.

entries.check_message: Tier is not declared in depfile file.
entries.note: Undeclared dependent tier %zzz: (neither %x* nor a chatter-supported standard tier). chatter rejects it via E605 UnsupportedDependentTier (Error severity). The supported set deliberately excludes the legacy %grt/%tra/%trn/%umor tiers retired with the UD-%mor consolidation, so chatter is intentionally stricter than CLAN there (documented divergence). %x* is always valid. CLAN CHECK 17.

entries.check_message: Illegal time representation.
entries.note: @Time Duration 99:99:99 is shape-valid (HH:MM:SS) but out of clock range. chatter previously validated the shape only; now TimeDurationValue::has_out_of_range_component flags components outside hours 0-23 / minutes,seconds 0-59 and emits E540. CLAN range rule grounded empirically: 25:00:00, 99:30:00, 12:60:00, 12:00:60 all CLAN 35; 23:59:59 OK. CLAN CHECK 35.

entries.check_message: Please remove "unlinked" from @Media header.
entries.note: @Media declares `unlinked` but the main tier has a time bullet, so the media is in fact linked. chatter now emits E552 MediaUnlinkedWithTiming via check_media_unlinked_has_no_timing, the inverse of the existing E544 (linkage without timing). Grounded empirically: unlinked+bullet -> CLAN 124; unlinked+no-bullet and linked+bullet -> no 124. CLAN CHECK 124.

entries.check_message: Space character is required before "[" code item.
entries.note: A replacement `[: foo]` glued to a word with no preceding space (`word[: foo]`). Fixed at the GRAMMAR level: word_with_optional_annotations now REQUIRES `$.whitespaces` before the replacement (matching base_annotations, which already required it for scope codes like [?]/[/]), so the glued form is an ERROR node and the parser rejects it (E375 ContentAnnotationParseError). The spaced `word [: foo]` still validates clean. CLAN CHECK 161.

entries.check_message: Media file name has to match datafile name.
entries.note: @Media filename ('differentname') does not match the datafile basename. chatter HAS E531 MediaFilenameMismatch, but the check was DEAD via the CLI: the validation_runner passed `None` for the filename so it never ran (it only fired in the parity harness, which passes the stem, masking the gap). Fixed by threading the file stem through validate_single_file_streaming. CLAN exempts remote URL @Media (verified: `@Media: "https://..."` -> no 157), so E531 now exempts URLs too. CLAN CHECK 157.

entries.check_message: Illegal colon on a header that takes no colon (@Begin).
entries.note: `@Begin:` (a stray colon on the no-colon @Begin header) parses as a valid @Begin followed by a stray `:` ERROR node. The streaming lowering silently dropped that nested ERROR node, so chatter accepted the file (Valid:1) while CLAN rejects it (codes 5 and 6). A whole-tree recovery-node backstop in parse_lines_with_old_tree now surfaces any surviving ERROR node as E316 UnparsableContent (recovery is not validity); the AST is still produced. CLAN CHECK 5 (co-emitted with 6).

entries.check_message: @Begin header missing or malformed.
entries.note: `@Begins` (a misspelled @Begin) parses as @Begin plus a trailing `s` ERROR node that the streaming lowering silently dropped. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN rejects it (codes 6 and 17). CLAN CHECK 6 (co-emitted with 17).

entries.check_message: A code must be entirely on one line.
entries.note: A scoped code split across a newline (`[% comment` then a continuation line `continued]`) leaves an ERROR node in the main-tier content that the streaming lowering did not scan. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN CHECK 106.

entries.check_message: Postcodes must precede the final bullet.
entries.note: A postcode `[+ trn]` placed after the final time bullet leaves an ERROR node in the utterance that the streaming lowering did not scan. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN rejects it (codes 108 and 112). CLAN CHECK 108 (co-emitted with 112).

entries.check_message: "@End" is missing at the end of the file.
entries.note: File has no @End header at all. CLAN 7 (@End missing at end of file); chatter E502 MissingEndHeader.

entries.check_message: Expected characters are: @ %% * TAB.
entries.note: A body line that does not begin with @, %, * or TAB (free text). CLAN 8 (expected @ %% * TAB); chatter E326 surfaces the unparsable main-tier start.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ] found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched ] on a main tier. CLAN 23; chatter rejects via E375 (content-annotation parse error).

entries.check_message: Unmatched > found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched > on a main tier. CLAN 25; chatter surfaces the tree-sitter recovery node as E316 UnparsableContent.

entries.check_message: Unmatched } found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched } on a main tier. CLAN 27 (also 48); chatter surfaces the recovery node as E316 UnparsableContent.

entries.check_message: The file must end with "@End" tier.
entries.note: Speaker content after the @End tier. CLAN 44 (file must end with @End); chatter flags E502 MissingEndHeader (and E501). Both reject the file.

entries.check_message: Numbers are not allowed inside words.
entries.note: A digit inside a word (he11o). CLAN 47 (numbers not allowed inside words); chatter E220.

entries.check_message: "@ID:" tier is missing in the file. Please run "insert" in Commands window on this data file.
entries.note: File has no @ID header. CLAN 60; chatter E522 (missing @ID header).

entries.check_message: The UTF8 header is missing. If you edit and save the file, it will be inserted.
entries.note: File has no @UTF8 header. CLAN 69; chatter E503 MissingUTF8Header.

entries.check_message: "@Languages:" tier is expected here.
entries.note: File has no @Languages header. CLAN 77 (also 61); chatter E504 MissingRequiredHeader. chatter uses one generic missing-required-header code where CLAN has per-header codes; parity is on validity.

entries.check_message: Found a space character instead of TAB character after Tier name
entries.note: A space instead of a TAB after the speaker code (*CHI: hi). CLAN 4; chatter requires the tab in-grammar and surfaces the recovery node as E316.

entries.check_message: Spaces before tier code.
entries.note: Leading spaces before the *tier code. CLAN 14 (also 4); chatter E326.

entries.check_message: Only one occurrence of | symbol per word is allowed.
entries.note: A %mor word with two | separators (n|dog|cat). CLAN 79 (only one | per word); chatter surfaces the malformed %mor structure as E316.

entries.check_message: There must be at least one occurrence of '|'.
entries.note: A %mor word with no | separator (bare 'dog'). CLAN 80 (must be at least one |); chatter E316 (also E702).

entries.check_message: Special quote U2019 must be replaced by single quote (').
entries.note: A right single curly quote U+2019 inside a word. CLAN 138; chatter E256 IllegalCurlyQuote (both parsers reject U+2018/U+2019).

entries.check_message: Special quote U2018 must be replaced by single quote (').
entries.note: A left single curly quote U+2018 at the start of a word. CLAN 139; chatter E256 IllegalCurlyQuote.

entries.check_message: Language on @ID tier is not defined on "@Languages:" header tier.
entries.note: @ID declares language 'eng' that is not present in the @Languages header (which declares 'fra'). CLAN 122; chatter E519. NOTE: code 122 has n_call_sites=0 in the generated catalogue (emitted via an indirect call the literal check_err grep misses) but IS emittable: verified vs real file-mode CLAN. The 8-code 'dead' list therefore needs empirical re-verification.

entries.check_message: Illegal character. Please re-enter it using Unicode standard.
entries.note: A Unicode Private Use Area character (U+E000) inside a word. CLAN 86 via isIllegalASCII (check.cpp:2880): 3-byte UTF-8 codepoints U+E000..U+FFFF except U+F170..U+F264 (CLAN-internal) and U+FF01..U+FF5E (fullwidth ASCII); U+00B7 allowed. chatter rejects it via E243 IllegalCharactersInWord (check_word_characters extends its illegal-char scan to the private-use ranges).

entries.check_message: Blank lines are not allowed.
Unmatched < found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched < on a main tier (open angle, no close). CLAN 24 (also 160); chatter surfaces the recovery node as E316. NOTE: earlier wrongly recorded as not-firing; that was a one-off CLAN depfile-load miss, the helper now guards against it.

entries.check_message: Unmatched { found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched { on a main tier. CLAN 26 (also 11, 48); chatter E316.

entries.check_message: Wrong gender information (Choose: female or male).
entries.note: @ID sex field is neither 'male' nor 'female' ('x'). CLAN 64; chatter E542 UnsupportedSex. Requires CLAN's depfile loaded (auto-resolved from the lib dir).

entries.check_message: Either illegal SES field value or symbol is not declared in depfile.
entries.note: @ID SES field is an undeclared value ('XYZ'). CLAN 144 (illegal SES / not declared in depfile); chatter E546 UnsupportedSesValue.

entries.check_message: Age's month or day are missing initial zero. Please run "chstring +q +1" command on this file to fix this error.
entries.note: @ID age with a component missing its leading zero (2;6. instead of 2;06.). CLAN 153; chatter E517 InvalidAgeFormat.

entries.check_message: Illegal date representation.
entries.note: @Date is not a valid date ('notadate'). CLAN 34 (illegal date representation); chatter E518 InvalidDateFormat.

entries.check_message: Unmatched [ found on the tier.
entries.note: An unclosed replacement scope: `dog [: cat` opens `[:` with no closing `]`. CLAN 22 (unmatched [) (also 11/48/106); chatter rejects with E311. A stray `[`+space alone is CLAN 48, not 22; the bracket must open a real scope to be 'unmatched'.

entries.check_message: Undeclared special form marker in depfile.
entries.note: An undeclared special-form marker `foo@zzz`. CLAN CHECK 147. ROOT CAUSE was the parser conversion, NOT the grammar: the grammar intentionally consumes `@zzz` as one form_marker token (parse-don't-validate), but the conversion's `else if text.starts_with("@z")` branch treated any `@z...` as the user-defined form, stripping `@z` and using the rest as a bare label without the required colon. Fixed by requiring the `@z:` colon (the only valid user-defined form is `@z:label`); `@zXXX` without the colon now falls through to the existing E203 InvalidFormType branch in both the tree-sitter and re2c conversions. chatter rejects it via E203.

entries.check_message: Illegal pause format. Pause has to have '.'
entries.note: An illegal pause format: `(5)` (digits with no dot) instead of (.)/(..)/(...)/(n.). CLAN 111 (pause must have '.'); chatter rejects with E209 (also E220).

entries.check_message: Postcodes are not allowed on dependent tiers.
entries.note: A postcode-shaped token `[+ trn]` on a dependent tier (%com). CLAN CHECK 109 ("Postcodes are not allowed on dependent tiers", check_CheckWords, check.cpp:3471-3690) flags it on any non-%x dependent tier. chatter does not model postcodes on dependent tiers: ordinary dependent tiers like %com parse as TextTier with no Postcode AST node, so there is nothing typed to detect, and flagging it would require a banned raw-text scan. Empirically (2026-06-25) CLAN's own analysis tools do not choke on dependent-tier postcodes: FREQ and MLU exclude the `[+ ...]` token from counts exactly as on the main tier, KWAL displays the line; only CHECK flags it. CHECK 109 is a CLAN-internal pedantic check, not a CHAT-validity rule, so chatter deliberately diverges (accepts) per the parity mandate's 'unless CHECK is buggy / not a real validity rule' clause. Divergence (chatter intentionally looser), not a gap.
